title: The Chronicles of Pinecone Mouse and the Pink Tow Truck
summary: Pinecone Mouse and her friends, Plotty, Mischief, and Mistress, enjoy a feast of vegetable soup and go for a walk in the woods to find a special stick. Later, Mistress Mouse takes her pink tow truck on a long journey through the city to the cake store. Equipped with crayons, snacks, and a purple CD of Sesame Street songs, she returns home to decorate a cake and settle in for a night of rest with her family.

highlightQuote.text: Those songs, the Sesame Street songs are good for mice. They love those songs. It's the best.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Here's the stars. Now it's almost time to go to sleep, so we can do a little bit of stars, and then it'll be time to go to sleep. I'm gonna turn out the light.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You want to sit on my lap?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm not going to read a book page. No book pages are in this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No book pages? Okay. What should we tell a story about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A what of what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what's the mouse's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. Pinecone mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Pinecone Mouse. Okay. Once upon a time, Pinecone Mouse woke up in her tiny house high up in a pine tree. And she was very hungry. So what did she decide to do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She tried to go to the groce— she did go to the grocery store to buy some vegetable soup.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and she bought some vegetable soup, and she brought it home, but she realized she had so much vegetable soup, it was more than she could eat. So she decided to invite over some friends. Who did she invite over?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some more pinecone mice.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what were their names?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. Plotty Mouse and Mischief Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, she invited over Plotty Mouse and Mischief Mouse, the pinecone mice. And everyone had—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Mistress Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Mistress Mouse also. And all the pinecone mice had a delicious meal of vegetable soup. And then they decided to go out for a walk together. And so they went walking in the woods. And all of a sudden they were surprised because they saw—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A stick!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A stick.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And a knocked-over tree.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And a knocked-over tree. And then what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They bringed the stick home and they left the tree outside, and they just brought home the stick.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they brought home the stick. And what did they do with it at home?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They put it somewhere safe so their cat stayed— so their friends' Mama Mouse could see it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's a good idea. You don't want your cat—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Dad Mouse could see it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Do another book about Mistress Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's make up another story about Mistress Mouse. One more and then it's time for bed. Once upon a time, Mistress Mouse was driving through the city in her little pink tow truck.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: With her crayons and paper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, why did she bring her crayons and paper this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because her dad was— because, because they wanted to go to the cake store for some sprinkles and cake, so they had to drive the cake store with sprinkles and cake. It's a long drive.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it was a long drive to the cake store to get sprinkles and cake. And so they brought their crayons and paper so they could draw in the car on the way? Um, what else did they do in the car on the way?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. They ate and drank on the way too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's a good idea. They had—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They had those in their bag, and snacks too, and a cupcake.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And there were no cakes left.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And did they sing any songs?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, they had the CD and they listened to the song on the— it was purple.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What was purple?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The CD was purple.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what CD was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a CD for mice.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A CD for mice. Oh. What music did it have on it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It had lots of kinds of music.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what was the best song on it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Those songs, the Sesame Street songs are good for mice. They love those songs. It's the best.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well anyway, Mistress Mouse made it to the cake store and got her cake and sprinkles, and then she got back in her car and she drove all the way home. And then what did she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She decorated it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and then what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She played with her mom and dad and soon they had to rest.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Soon they had to rest.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then they was resting. Her mama was playing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And then everyone brushed their teeth and changed into their pajamas and read a story and sang a lullaby and then they got into their beds. And then what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I think they went to sleep. Zzzzz.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now that's the end.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now that's the end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now time to turn off the stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's turn off the stars. Thanks for doing stars and stories, Izzy.

characters.name: Pinecone Mouse
characters.description: As tall as a garden wall and driven by an insatiable hunger, this colossal mouse dwells within a tiny, precarious house perched among the highest needles of a swaying pine tree.

characters.name: Plotty Mouse
characters.description: A wandering soul with a penchant for long rambles and the crisp snap of garden carrots. A loyal companion to Pinecone Mouse, they are a spirited fixture at every vegetable soup feast, relishing the steam of a shared pot and the earthy sweetness of harvested roots.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

characters.name: Mistress Mouse
characters.description: A polite three-year-old mouse and resourceful shopkeeper, this community cornerstone balances a bold artistic spirit with sharp mechanical grit. Whether crafting intricate Lego creations in her sunroom or piloting her signature pink tow truck to solve puzzles and assist neighbors, she is a master of the road and a multi-talented creator. From famously adorning cars with blue stripes to dominating the race track, her inventive soul is always on the move. Usually seen in a mushroom-adorned cardboard hat with an orange sparkle purse, this resourceful driver remains an essential, evocative fixture of every neighborhood adventure.

characters.name: Mama Mouse
characters.description: The devoted matriarch of the Blind Mice family and watchful guardian of the household, she balances the sweet lilt of nightly lullabies with the adrenaline of a seasoned racer. Behind the wheel of her medium-sized black car—carrying a well-thumbed book on the secrets of junk—she guards Hattie and Avery’s peace as fiercely as she commands the asphalt.

characters.name: Dad Mouse
characters.description: A devoted patriarch who flourishes in the company of his kin, Surprise Mouse’s father is her most steadfast creative collaborator, lending a patient hand to her artistic projects while cherishing the simple warmth of shared family time.

places.name: Pine Tree House
places.description: The elevated home where Pinecone Mouse wakes up.

places.name: Grocery Store
places.description: A distant neighborhood shop stocked with soda, crisp celery, and jewel-toned pomegranates, forever etched in the trio’s memory for the bananas they gathered and the messy remains of squished raspberries. It remains a whimsical local sanctuary where, amidst the nostalgia, even the resident mice can be found purchasing small bowls of hearty vegetable soup.

places.name: The Woods
places.description: A forested area where the mice find a fallen tree and a special stick.

places.name: The City
places.description: An urban area that Mistress Mouse navigates in her pink tow truck.

places.name: Cake Store
places.description: A shop a long drive away that sells cakes and sprinkles.
